elements.title: a statue in London
elements.contents: ... that the first British writer to openly support the Thirteen Colonies in the American Revolutionary War was later honoured with a statue in London (pictured)?

elements.title: Frederick S. Spiegel
elements.contents: ... that Cincinnati mayor Frederick S. Spiegel spent his final years as legal counsel for the city's subway project, during which an inspection accident ultimately led to his death?

elements.title: Fly, Fly My Sadness
elements.contents: ... that the combination of Bulgarian choral music and Tuvan throat singing on Fly, Fly My Sadness was inspired by multiple albums being played at the same time?

elements.title: River Moon
elements.contents: ... that River Moon was a mortician's assistant and managed a meme page before pursuing a DJ career?

elements.title: Phạm Đoan Trang
elements.contents: ... that Vietnamese writer Phạm Đoan Trang asked for permission to keep her guitar while in jail?

elements.title: a Florida man in his 70s
elements.contents: ... that a Florida man in his 70s claims an internet following of Gen Zers?

elements.title: Anna Van Patten
elements.contents: ... that Anna Van Patten learned pole dancing to prepare for her role in Euphoria?

elements.title: Macrocarpaea dies-viridis
elements.contents: ... that Macrocarpaea dies-viridis was named after the band Green Day?

elements.title: John W. Townsend Jr.
elements.contents: ... that John W. Townsend Jr. used rockets to measure atmospheric gas over a subarctic town?
